name: [CM] REC | 2. Analyse Salesforce Case Details and Sync to Jira Ticket

extended_input_schema.name: since_offset

extended_input_schema.hint:
Provide comma separated issue ids to get consistent results immediately after updating the issues. Accepts maximum 50 ids.
Note: Consistency is ensured only for specified issues.
extended_input_schema.label: Reconcile Issue IDs
extended_input_schema.name: reconcileIssues

input.question: Analyze the sentiment of the description to Positivem Neutral or Negative.

extended_input_schema.hint: Provide issue ID or key of parent issue, e.g. 10105 (issue ID) or ABC-123 (issue key).Use issue ID if your issue moves between projects.
extended_input_schema.label: Parent
extended_input_schema.name: parent

extended_input_schema.hint: Enter request participants separated by commas. If the values have comma in it, make sure to give it as array of string using formula mode. For example: ["A,B,C", "D,E,F"]
extended_input_schema.label: Request participants
extended_input_schema.name: customfield_10037

extended_input_schema.hint: Enter affected services separated by commas. If the values have comma in it, make sure to give it as array of string using formula mode. For example: ["A,B,C", "D,E,F"]

extended_input_schema.hint: Enter approver groups separated by commas. If the values have comma in it, make sure to give it as array of string using formula mode. For example: ["A,B,C", "D,E,F"]
extended_input_schema.label: Approver groups

extended_input_schema.hint: Enter organizations separated by commas. If the values have comma in it, make sure to give it as array of string using formula mode. For example: ["A,B,C", "D,E,F"]
